assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and, for Node.js routes, Node.js 16+.
No Replicate account, API token, or billing setup exist yet.
No Get started runtime among Node.js, Google Colab, or Python has been preselected.

developer_goal: Create a Replicate account, obtain an API token, and run a public model through one official Get started route until the documented success output appears.

first_success.outcome: Depends on the unresolved runtime; for the Node.js FLUX.1 Schnell guide, the script saves output.png after running the model.
first_success.observable_signal: For the manual Node.js path, the terminal prints Running the model then Image saved as output.png and output.png exists.

route_selection.rule: Node.js, Google Colab, and Python are separate top-level run-model routes with no universal default; fine-tune and deploy are other first outcomes.

candidates.name: npx create-replicate scaffold for FLUX.1 Schnell
candidates.reason: Documented fastest Node path, but still requires account/token/billing gates that are not fully enumerated.

candidates.name: Manual Node.js project from scratch
candidates.reason: Equal documented Node outcome with more local setup steps than create-replicate.

candidates.name: Google Colab or Python get-started
candidates.reason: Different runtimes and success signals with equal top-level docs placement.

candidates.name: Fine-tune or deploy custom model
candidates.reason: Different first-success type from running a public model.

required_path.action: Choose the intended first outcome and Get started runtime route.
required_path.observable_result: One official run-model route is selected.

required_path.action: Create a Replicate account or log in.
required_path.observable_result: The developer can open API tokens and billing settings.

required_path.action: If prompted for the selected model or account, complete billing setup required to continue running predictions.

required_fields.label: Billing setup controls
required_fields.notes: Docs say some models run free until limits, after which billing setup is required; timing is account-dependent.

required_path.observable_result: The account is allowed to run the selected prediction.

required_path.action: Open API tokens and copy the default token or create and copy a dedicated token.
For the Node.js manual route: export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN, scaffold the project, install replicate, add index.js, and run node index.js.
required_path.observable_result: The terminal prints Image saved as output.png and the file exists.

prerequisites.description: Node.js 16 or greater for the Node.js get-started routes.

external_gates.description: A Replicate account and API token are required to authenticate predictions.
Billing setup may be required after free limits or for some features.

unavoidable_waits.description: Model instances may cold-boot while setting up before becoming active.

platform_outcomes.description: Replicate runs the selected model prediction and returns generated output such as an image file.
Public model billing charges only active processing time once the account is billable.

excluded.item: Fine-tune an image model and Deploy a custom model cards
excluded.reason: Different first-success types from running a public model.

excluded.item: Next.js website and other follow-on guides
excluded.reason: Listed after the Node.js first-run success.

sources.title: Replicate Documentation
evidence_supported: Get started cards for Node, Colab, Python, fine-tune, deploy

sources.title: Run a model from Google Colab

uncertainties.question: Which official Replicate Get started route should define the canonical workshop journey?
uncertainties.impact: Runtime, installation, external notebook auth, and success signals all change.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An explicit choice among Node.js create-replicate, manual Node.js, Colab, and Python, or an official recommended default.

uncertainties.question: What exact account signup screens and billing prompts appear for a fresh user before the first FLUX prediction succeeds?
uncertainties.impact: Signup fields and whether billing is required before first success remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough from signup through first successful prediction, including any billing interstitial.
